mechanism_narrative: Sustained low unemployment indicates a strong labor market, supporting consumer spending and corporate profits, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:low unemployment generally correlates with economic growth
failure_condition: If UNRATE remains below 5% for 6 consecutive months, but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are consistently negative over that period.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate exceeds a certain threshold, it signals restrictive monetary policy, increasing borrowing costs for corporations and consumers, which negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:higher rates increase cost of capital
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS consistently stays above 4.5% for 6 months, but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are predominantly positive in more than 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong retail sales and robust housing starts indicates strong consumer demand and investment, signaling broad economic health that supports S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month average growth > 0.5% AND HOUST 3-month average growth > 1%
evidence_refs: fact:consumer spending and housing are key economic drivers
failure_condition: If both RSAFS and HOUST show sustained growth above thresholds for 3 consecutive months, but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic contraction, a decline in industrial production signals weakening corporate output and profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If INDPRO declines by more than 0.5% for three consecutive months during a declared recession, but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in non-farm payrolls above a certain monthly threshold indicates robust job creation, boosting consumer confidence and spending, which supports S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:strong job growth typically leads to higher equity valuations
failure_condition: If PAYEMS consistently adds over 200,000 jobs per month for 3 months, yet S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of these instances.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent high inflation erodes purchasing power and corporate margins, leading to expectations of tighter monetary policy and negatively impacting S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:high inflation is generally detrimental to equity valuations
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL annual growth remains above 4% for 6 consecutive months, but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are predominantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth is strong but accompanied by high inflation, it suggests an overheating economy that may force the central bank to tighten aggressively, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annual growth > 3% AND CPIAUCSL annual growth > 4%
evidence_refs: fact:overheating economies often lead to policy tightening
failure_condition: If GDPC1 and CPIAUCSL both exceed their respective thresholds for two consecutive quarters, but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic stress, a rising unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer confidence, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:rising unemployment is a classic recessionary indicator
failure_condition: If UNRATE increases by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months during a period of economic contraction, but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive.
